finding: When an agent opens or imports a framework-development task from inside `AI-Atomic-Framework`, ATM does not remind them to first author the plan MD and formal task card under the canonical 3KLife planning root (`docs/ai_atomic_framework/**/tasks/`). Agents can therefore claim/import/close using only `.atm/task-plans/` in the target repo, leaving the AAO/TEAM planning roster incomplete until someone notices later.
expectedBehavior: For framework-repo task creation/import/open routes, ATM should detect missing planning-root authorship and emit a blocking or high-priority notice with the exact 3KLife path pattern and next command (for example write `.../atm-agent-first-operability/tasks/TASK-AAO-NNNN-....task.md`, update the roster README, then import from that path). Target-repo-only task-plans should be treated as incomplete for AAO/TEAM-style work unless an explicit waiver is recorded.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed in TASK-AAO-0193: framework-repo `tasks import` of AAO/TEAM cards from `.atm/task-plans/` now fail-closed on `--write` (and warn on `--dry-run`) unless a matching planning-root card exists or `--waive-planning-root --reason` is explicit. Regression: `packages/cli/src/commands/tasks/__tests__/planning-root-authorship.spec.ts`.
followUp: Keep the same preflight visible on taskflow open guidance when AAO/TEAM work starts from target-only mirrors.
